macro_events.event_summary: Trump pauses a U.S. naval initiative to escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz, citing progress on an Iran nuclear/sanctions deal — signaling de-escalation in the Persian Gulf.
primary_drivers: Lower geopolitical risk premium in crude oil
Potential return of Iranian barrels to global supply if sanctions ease
Reduced shipping risk/insurance costs through Hormuz chokepoint

beneficiaries.rationale: Delta has minimal fuel hedging and is highly sensitive to jet fuel/crude prices; a $5/bbl drop in crude flows directly to margin. De-escalation removes Hormuz risk premium.
United similarly under-hedged on fuel; lower crude from Iran de-escalation directly reduces its largest variable cost line.
Southwest historically hedged but exposure remains material; lower crude from easing Gulf tensions improves cost outlook for a fuel-sensitive domestic carrier.
Ethylene/petrochemical feedstock costs fall with lower crude and naphtha; Dow's commodity chemical margins expand on input cost relief.

losers.rationale: Integrated major with massive upstream exposure; lower crude price from Iran deal progress + potential Iranian barrel return directly compresses E&P realizations.
Same upstream sensitivity as XOM; pure crude price taker on majority of production. Iran deal raises supply, lowers price deck.
Permian-heavy pure-play E&P with high beta to WTI; removal of Hormuz risk premium and prospect of Iranian supply directly hits revenue per barrel.
Diamondback is a near-pure-play Permian E&P with no downstream offset; highest WTI sensitivity among large caps to a geopolitical risk-off in crude.
Oilfield services demand tracks E&P capex, which falls with lower crude; Halliburton's North American completions business is most exposed to a sustained price drop.
International services leader; lower crude prices and a potential Iran sanctions easing reshuffle pricing power and could compress Middle East service rates.

Frontline VLCC tanker rates are partly supported by Hormuz risk premia and longer-haul re-routing; de-escalation compresses rates and Iranian barrels coming legal shortens trade routes vs. shadow fleet.
Lockheed defense demand has a geopolitical premium component; visible Iran de-escalation reduces near-term urgency for missile defense/munitions reorders that had been bid up.

headlines: Trump pauses U.S. bid to guide ships out of Strait of Hormuz, cites Iran deal progress
